summary: Large item-system decompilation pass for src/melee/it/items/itkusudama.c, replacing many placeholder stubs with C for Kusudama spawn, motion, collision, damage, drop/throw, effects, and item-spawning behavior. The PR description says everything matched except it_80289BE8, reported at 97%, with the author suspecting further refactoring was needed. Supporting headers gained Kusudama item-vars and special-attribute structs, itkusudama.h prototypes were corrected, and it_8026F3AC was changed from void to returning ItemKind so random item spawning could be represented in C.
what_changed: Implemented most of itkusudama.c, adding about 555 lines and replacing placeholder markers for functions including it_802896CC, it_3F14_Logic4_Spawned, it_802897C8, it_80289910, it_80289A00, it_80289B50, it_80289BE8, it_8028A114, it_8028A190, it_8028A3CC, it_8028A544, it_8028AC74, it_8028AD44, and several itKusudama_UnkMotion and Logic4 callbacks.
Added itKusudama_ItemVars to itCommonItems.h with fields mapped to ip+DD4 and ip+DD8, used through ip->xDD4_itemVar.kusudama.x0 and x4.

Added itKusudamaAttributes to itCommonItems.h with probability/count/physics-style fields x0, x4, x8, xC, x10, x14, x18, x1C, x20, x28, and x2C; these are read from ip->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes.
Changed it_8026F3AC in it_266F.c/.h from void to ItemKind and returned it_8026C75C(&it_804A0E50), enabling callers in it_80289BE8 to consume a random item kind.

Updated itkusudama.h prototypes from unknown or incorrect signatures toward concrete signatures, including it_802897C8(Item_GObj*, f32), it_80289910(Item_GObj*, f32), it_80289A00(Item_GObj*), it_80289B50(Item_GObj*, s32), and it_80289BE8(Item_GObj*, s32, s32, s32).
Replaced a suspicious old extern f32 it_804DC9E4 call pattern with explicit gobj-plus-angle calls such as it_802897C8(gobj, 1.5707964f).

Added local static inline helpers in itkusudama.c to model duplicated compiler output patterns, including it_80289BE8_inline, it_80289BE8_spawn, it_8028A190_inline, it_8028A544_inline, itKusudama_UnkMotion3_inline, collision helpers, and damage/clank helpers.

Implemented Kusudama item spawning logic with SpawnItem initialization for It_Kind_Kusudama and later contents spawning for configured item kind, food, or random items, including HSD_Randi/HSD_Randf velocity randomization and ftLib_800874CC owner notification.

Implemented motion/collision transitions that reset velocity, set animation states via Item_80268E5C, update rotations with HSD_JObjGet/SetRotationX/Y, call item physics helpers, and set Kusudama state flags/timers.
Implemented burst/effect behavior in itKusudama_UnkMotion8_Anim, calling it_80289BE8 and spawning effect 0x433 on dynamic bones 3 and 5.
smart_moves: The PR introduced item-specific vars and attributes before relying on them in code, making the Kusudama behavior expressible through ip->xDD4_itemVar.kusudama and specialAttributes rather than raw offsets.

Changing it_8026F3AC to return ItemKind was a targeted prototype correction: the implementation already called it_8026C75C, and Kusudama needed the selected kind to fill spawned[] and pass it to it_80289BE8_spawn.

Factoring repeated position/velocity setup into it_80289BE8_inline and spawn setup into it_80289BE8_spawn reduced repeated code in the difficult contents-spawning function while preserving evidence of the common assembly pattern.
The implementation kept small static inline transition helpers for repeated animation/rotation state changes, a common matching tactic when the compiler duplicated short sequences across callbacks.

The PR used concrete math constants for rotations and angle clamps, such as 1.5707964f, 3.1415927f, 6.2831855f, 0.17453292f, and 0.08726646f, instead of abstracting them away, which is often necessary for float-sensitive matching.
The author explicitly documented that it_80289BE8 was only 97% matched, preserving the known weak point rather than overstating completion.
decomp_lessons: For item files, matching often depends on first correcting common helper prototypes; if a helper semantically returns an ItemKind, leaving it as void can block accurate caller reconstruction.

Item-specific xDD4 vars should be modeled in itCommonItems.h when multiple callbacks share state flags/timers; this turns raw offset manipulation into stable field references and improves future matching.
Special-attribute structs can be introduced with placeholder names and offsets when semantics are not fully known; preserving exact field offsets is more important than forcing speculative names.
When an item behavior repeatedly transitions animation states, small static inline helpers can mirror the compiler's duplicated code and improve matching without creating exported symbols.
For hard functions like it_80289BE8, isolate repeated sub-expressions such as position/velocity setup and spawn wrappers, then compare whether call sites are equivalent to those helpers.
Do not hide unresolved matching work: recording that it_80289BE8 was 97% and likely needed refactoring is useful for future decomp passes.

Callbacks for damage, clank, shield hit, reflection, dropped, thrown, and collision may share core transition helpers; matching can improve by identifying these behavioral aliases instead of independently writing each callback.
naming_conventions: New item-specific structs followed the project pattern itKusudama_ItemVars and itKusudamaAttributes.
Unknown struct fields were kept offset-based, e.g. x0, x4, x8, xC, x10, x14, x18, x1C, x20, x28, x2C, with comments documenting offsets.
Item-var access used the established union style ip->xDD4_itemVar.kusudama.x0 and ip->xDD4_itemVar.kusudama.x4.
Local helper names used the target function address plus suffixes like _inline, _spawn, _inline2, and callback-specific names such as itKusudama_UnkMotion5_Coll_inline.
Existing unresolved function names remained address-based, e.g. it_80289BE8 and it_8028A114, rather than inventing semantic names without evidence.
Item kind constants were expressed with existing enum-style names such as It_Kind_Kusudama, It_Kind_Foods, and It_Kind_M_Ball.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK annotations with specific byte counts, including PAD_STACK(4), PAD_STACK(8), and PAD_STACK(12), to preserve stack layout for matching.
Introduced static inline helpers to coerce compiler inlining and duplicate short transition sequences where the assembly likely expected inlined code.
Kept local variable shadowing in it_80289BE8, including inner Vec3 pos/vel declarations, which may have been needed for stack/register layout in the difficult 97% function.
Used goto food_or_random in it_80289BE8 to represent branch structure for the contents-selection logic.
Preserved explicit float constants and arithmetic order in rotation and velocity calculations to avoid float-codegen drift.
Used boolean and enum return values, especially ItemKind return values with -1 sentinel, to match control flow in item spawn paths.
Compared gobj-spawn results against NULL before applying velocity and effect/notification calls, mirroring object creation control flow.
Reviewer/author comment identified a potential refactor: an explicit zeroed pos/vel block appeared suspiciously equivalent to it_80289BE8_inline(gobj, 0.0f, &pos, &vel).

review_feedback.author: lukechampine
review_feedback.lesson: In the remaining hard function, try replacing explicit zero-velocity setup with the shared inline helper at vel_scale 0.0f; the equivalence may improve structure or reveal why the match is still off.

review_feedback.author: lukechampine
review_feedback.comment: Everything except `it_80289BE8` (97%), which presumably just needs some refactoring; I tried a bunch of stuff but this was the closest I could get it :/
review_feedback.lesson: The known weak match is it_80289BE8; future work should focus on refactoring, not broad behavior changes.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that mention it_80289BE8 or itkusudama.c to see whether the remaining 97% function was fully matched.
Search for later renames or semantic field names for itKusudamaAttributes fields x0, x4, x8, xC, x10, x14, x18, x1C, x20, x28, and x2C.
Check whether it_8026F3AC returning ItemKind affected other item files or required downstream prototype cleanups.
Look for future review comments about replacing explicit zero pos/vel setup with it_80289BE8_inline(gobj, 0.0f, &pos, &vel).
Compare the static inline helper patterns in itkusudama.c with other container item decompilations such as barrels, capsules, party balls, or food spawning.
